records.ety: [L. laus, laudis. See Laud, v. i.]

defs: High commendation; praise; honor; exaltation; glory.
quotes: So do well and thou shalt have laud of the same.

defs: A part of divine worship, consisting chiefly of praise; -- usually in the pl.
notes: ☞ In the Roman Catholic Church, the prayers used at daybreak, between those of matins and prime, are called lauds.

defs: Music or singing in honor of any one.

records.ety: [L. laudare, fr. laus, laudis, praise. Cf. Allow.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Lauded; p. pr. & vb. n. Lauding.]

defs: To praise in words alone, or with words and singing; to celebrate; to extol.
quotes: With all the company of heaven, we laud and magnify thy glorious name.
